_comment: Canonical wire envelope for the OPERATOR_HEARTBEAT_RECEIVED SSE event pushed from g8ee to g8ed (and fanned out to the browser). Authorship split: g8eo authors the telemetry inside `metrics`; g8ee owns the envelope (`operator_id`, `status`). Producers: g8ee `HeartbeatSSEEnvelope` (components/g8ee/app/models/operators.py). Consumers: g8ed `EventService` forwards the envelope unchanged to the browser; the browser reads `data.operator_id`, `data.status`, and `data.metrics.*`.

operator_id.description: Operator identifier. Matches the `operator_id` field on the persisted OperatorDocument.

status.description: Authoritative operator status at the time g8ee emitted the envelope. See status.json operator.status. g8ee is the sole writer — never derive from heartbeat fields.

metrics.description: Telemetry snapshot authored by g8eo, projected by g8ee from the most recent OperatorHeartbeat.

metrics._description: Telemetry projected from OperatorHeartbeat. All fields are authored by g8eo (see wire/heartbeat.json) — g8ee only normalizes shape and names. No g8ee-owned state (status, routing, correlation) lives here.

timestamp.description: When g8ee received the source heartbeat.

_comment.ownership: Authorship boundary is enforced by the type split: HeartbeatMetrics (g8eo-authored telemetry) vs HeartbeatSSEEnvelope (g8ee-owned `operator_id` + `status` + `metrics`). Mutating `status` on the envelope after construction is explicitly disallowed — the authoritative value comes from OperatorDocument at the call site in heartbeat_service.py.
_comment.routing: Routing fields (`web_session_id`, `user_id`, `case_id`, `investigation_id`) are added by the surrounding SessionEvent/BackgroundEvent envelope in app/models/events.py. They are NOT part of this wire schema and must never be placed inside `metrics`.
